column1: # /Copy — Lotus tutorial behaviors: single-source replicate, same-size,
# formula relative-ref shift, mismatched-dim error, 3D destination.
# Tutorial uses `\-` to draw a horizontal rule across a row by typing
# a single repeating-dash label and copying it across.
# /C source = A1..A1, dest paint via `.` then RIGHT four times to F1.
# Move pointer to B1, anchor with `.`, extend to F1.
# Put a formula at A8 that sums A3+A4+A5; copy A8 to B8 and verify the
# formula now reads B3+B4+B5 (and evaluates against the values just
# written through their copy at C3..C5 — actually we use the originals).
# Seed B3..B5 with different values so the shift is observable.
# Copy A8 → B8; refs shift by (+1, 0).
# Seed E1..E2 so we can verify they're not touched on a bad copy.
# /C from A3..A5 (3 rows) to E1..F2 (2x2 = 4 cells, mismatched).
# In TO, navigate to E1, anchor, extend to F2.
# Now copy A:A3..A:A5 (3 numbers) to B:A3 (single anchor on sheet B).
# Sheet B should now have the same values at A3..A5.
